question_text: When a person eats food and their body converts it into energy for movement, what type of energy transformation occurs?

options.label: A
options.text: Chemical energy is transformed into kinetic energy and thermal energy

options.label: B
options.text: Thermal energy is transformed into chemical energy

options.label: C
options.text: Kinetic energy is transformed into potential energy

options.label: D
options.text: Gravitational potential energy is transformed into chemical energy

correct_answer: Chemical energy is transformed into kinetic energy and thermal energy
explanation: Food contains chemical energy in its molecules. When your body digests food, respiration in your cells releases this chemical energy. Your body uses this energy in two main ways: to power muscle contractions for movement (kinetic energy) and to keep your body warm (thermal energy).
